headlines.title: Rystad: U.S.-Iran Re-Escalation Could Drive Oil To $180 By August
headlines.summary: An acute re-escalation in the U.S.-Iran conflict and a prolonged blockage of the Strait of Hormuz could drive global crude oil prices to $180 per barrel by August, Jorge León, Head of Geopolitical Analysis at Rystad Energy, has projected on CNBC's Squawk Box Europe. According to

headlines.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Refinery Deep Behind Front Lines
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones targeted a Russian oil refinery hundreds of kilometers from the Ukrainian border, the latest in a campaign of deep strikes aimed at disrupting Russia’s oil industry. headlines.title: Pakistan Inflation Accelerates to 11.7% on Oil and Gas Import Shock
headlines.summary: The surge in international oil and gas prices amid the Iran war hiked Pakistan's inflation to the highest level in two years as energy import costs ballooned. Pakistan's general inflation jumped to 11.7% in May from a year earlier, accelerating from 10.9% annual inflation in Apri

headlines.title: Inside The Iran Deal That May Change Nothing — But Could Smash Oil Prices Anyway
headlines.summary: After more than three months of fighting and stop-start negotiations, Washington and Tehran are now reportedly on the verge of a deal that would end the conflict. headlines.title: Oil Surges Over 7% as Iran Halts U.S. Talks and Threatens Total Hormuz Closure
headlines.summary: UPDATED 10:20 EST, MONDAY JUNE 1ST: Oil prices exploded higher Monday, with U.S. crude leaping more than 7% after Iran’s state-affiliated media reported that Tehran is cutting off diplomatic channels with Washington and moving to fully block the strategic Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Russia Bans Jet Fuel Exports as Ukrainian Attacks Cripple Refining
headlines.summary: Russia is banning exports of jet fuel through November 30, 2026, as it looks to ensure domestic supply amid intensifying Ukrainian drone attacks on the Russian refining infrastructure.
